question_text: A light bulb uses 100 J of electrical energy. It produces 25 J of light energy and the rest is wasted as heat. What is the efficiency of the light bulb?
explanation: Efficiency = useful energy out ÷ total energy in × 100. So 25 ÷ 100 × 100 = 25%. This means only a quarter of the electrical energy becomes light; the rest becomes wasted heat energy.
